pattern: Checking a mirrored pair of positions is a constant-time comparison, so scanning indices from the front and stopping at the first match directly gives the smallest qualifying index.
hints: For each index i, the position it must be compared against is fixed and computable directly: the mirrored position counting from the end.
Scan indices starting from 0 upward, comparing each character to its mirrored counterpart, and stop as soon as you find one that matches.
The very middle index of an odd-length string is always compared against itself, so it always matches -- keep that in mind when reasoning about the smallest possible answer.

constraints.note: s consists of lowercase English letters

edge_inputs.description: even length string with no mirrored match anywhere
even length string whose very first pair already matches
odd length string where only the middle index matches
palindrome-like string where an early index matches by coincidence before the middle
all identical characters, every index matches
maximum length string with no match anywhere

stress.description: random lowercase strings at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
